overview: This is a Madagascar giraffe weevil (Trachelophorus giraffa), an insect endemic to Madagascar. The male weevil shown uses its exceptionally long neck to fight rivals for mates. Despite their bizarre appearance, they are completely harmless to humans. They are herbivores that feed on leaves and are often found in the island's eastern rainforests. Facebook +3 4 sites Probably the weirdest kind of bug I have ever encountered ...
